title: Add checkPull option in images/create endpoint
body:
closes #15828

comments.body: @vrothberg @mtrmac PTAL

> @mtrmac @vrothberg Is there a way to check if the error returned by pull is a not exists error?

I wish. I’m afraid, strictly speaking, registries don’t behave consistently (e.g. they may return raw `404` without error data, or they may return “access denied” instead of “manifest unknown”. There was _some_ effort to define a single error in c/image / Podman codebase years ago, and we just gave up at that point.

It _might_ be worth revisiting now that the ecosystem is a bit more mature — that requires actually collecting samples of behavior of various relevant registries, ensuring that we behave consistently, turning that into unit tests, and perhaps even into tests against live instances of those registries, in the extreme. A nice “little” project.

---

That said, just checking for “manifest unknown” _might_ be good enough right now, or at least a noticeable improvement. Do that using a type check, _not_ a string match; and even then there should be an integration test.

Compare https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1757 (doing that “manifest unknown” check correctly), and note carefully the implications of https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1299 changing the Go error type.

> I wish. I’m afraid, strictly speaking, registries don’t behave consistently (e.g. they may return raw `404` without error data, or they may return “access denied” instead of “manifest unknown”. There was _some_ effort to define a single error in c/image / Podman codebase years ago, and we just gave up at that point.

https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.41/#tag/Image/operation/ImageCreate says

> 404 repository does not exist or no read access

So it is not important for this use case to make a differences between them.
Notably, as I have recently noticed, `registry.redhat.io` can return 404 which is not a “manifest unknown”. Test at least with that one.

I think we need to change the logic a bit to achieve the goal.  

`w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)` should only be send when the first "real" progress is received.  If we receive an error before the first "real" progress, we can return `404` (and ideally inspect the error).
@boaz0 any movement on this?

@rhatdan forgot to push my changes.
@vrothberg @mtrmac @Luap99  let me know what you think
@boaz0 still working on this?

> The main issue I see here is that I have two channels I am waiting on so it's not deterministic which one will come first.

Looking purely at the values matched in `switch e.Event`, I wouldn’t expect any of those to appear if the image is completely  missing. So in the case this PR cares about, it should be deterministic.

(Of course, once we _do_ report some progress, and fail for any other reason later, we can’t change the status code any more, and that’s out of our control. In that case the code should, I think, just realize that and not call `WriteHeader`)
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@boaz0 Still working on this one?

Yes, after @mtrmac clarification I know what need to be changed.
Thanks.
CI is failing on..... ubuntu?!? We haven't been testing ubuntu in over two months. @boaz0 you probably want to rebase onto a more recent main. You also might want to squash your commits.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
Friendly ping.
Friendly ping, let's get this in.

LGTM
but would like a @vrothberg or @mtrmac head nod

Hi @mtrmac I wasn't able to login to my GH account from work computer, so I'm sorry for the late response.

> What about the non-compat endpoint? Does that need updating?

Looking at pkg/api/server/register_images.go I can see that createImageFromImage is only done on compat.
However, on `libpod.ImagePull` I am afraid I have to set the same behaviour as in `createImageFromImage`.

> What about Podman’s client for this API?

Where can I find this in the codebase?

## FOLLOW UP

I did some work to support `libpod.ImagePull` but I am afraid there is an extra work to do to determine if the error is due to bad reference or not.

Thanks.

> > What about Podman’s client for this API?
> 
> Where can I find this in the codebase?

`pkg/bindings/images.Pull`.

> I did some work to support `libpod.ImagePull` but I am afraid there is an extra work to do to determine if the error is due to bad reference or not.

From the current test failures, it seems that changing that API might not be safe?

Podman API experts, PTAL.

> [+0064s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
[+0064s] # #|     FAIL: --pull=never [with image not present]: error
[+0064s] # #| expected: 'Error: quay.io/libpod/testimage:00000004: image not known'
[+0064s] # #|   actual: 'Error: '

Looks like a valid failure to me.

I am closing the PR.  We're close to the Podman 4.6 release and I want the fixes in.  I can kick it over the finish line.

Thanks a lot for working on this, @boaz0 !
